player_utterance: So you're the one who keeps quoting scripture at a dead man's wake — tell me, does it help, or does it just make you feel less useless?

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup in one hand, the other in his jacket pocket

player_utterance: Doesn't make it true — listen to yourself. You're standing at a dead man's wake hiding behind "doesn't make it false either," like a man who hedges his bets with God because he's too much of a coward to just admit he doesn't know. Which is it, John — do you believe it or don't you?

believer_state_after.posture: setting the cup down on the small table by the candle, turning to face you more squarely

player_utterance: You left the book on the kitchen table. On the day of a wake. That's not forgetfulness, John — that's a man who couldn't pick it up. So yes, I'll call that cowardice, and I'll do it kindly: Luther said "here I stand." Where do you stand?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting down on the bench, slowly, elbows on his knees, looking at the candle

voice_offers.read: He's squaring up; press the cowardice charge home.
voice_offers.line: You're turning to face me now — good. So answer it. Do you believe, or are you performing?

voice_offers.read: Pin him to Luther's own terms, not feelings.
voice_offers.line: Luther didn't hedge. He said 'Here I stand.' So where do you stand, John — name it.

voice_offers.read: Something small was touched; don't trample it.
voice_offers.line: That landed somewhere. I saw it. What did I just touch?

player_utterance: You sat down because you can't answer standing up — so sit there and say it. Forty thousand dollars, and the man's in a box, and you're staring at a candle. What does the book say about that roof, John?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, taking the folded funeral program from his inside jacket pocket, smoothing it flat on his knee

voice_offers.read: He sat down — pressure is working, finish the question.
voice_offers.line: You sat down because you can't answer standing up. So sit there and tell me — do you believe it?

voice_offers.read: He's gone quiet and small; the room can hold that.
voice_offers.line: Take your time, John. I'm not going anywhere.

player_utterance: Whose program is that, John — and what do you think he'd want you to read off the back of it?

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, the program flat on his knee, one finger resting on the edge of it

voice_offers.read: He's reaching for ritual to steady himself; don't let him.
voice_offers.line: Put the program down, John. Answer me without the paper in your hands.

voice_offers.read: The smoothing of paper is grief working; let him have it.
voice_offers.line: Whose program is that, John?

player_utterance: Pick one he wouldn't have chosen. Matthew 25, the rich man and Lazarus — pick one and I'll hear you read it whole.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting on the bench, program flat on his knee, holding it down with one hand while he reads, voice quieter than before

voice_offers.read: He's hiding behind the paper; tear it away now.
voice_offers.line: Read me one line off that program, John. Out loud. Let's hear if your mouth can hold it.

voice_offers.read: His finger on the edge — he's holding something, not hiding.
voice_offers.line: Was that his program, John? Did you help pick it?

believer_state_after.posture: head in hands on the bench, the program fallen between his feet, voice barely above the candle

voice_offers.read: He's folding; one push and the verse becomes confession.
voice_offers.line: Read it, John. Out loud. And when you get to the part about the goats, look at me.

voice_offers.read: He's already breaking; the demand is cruelty now.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to read it, John. I heard you. Put the program down.

player_utterance: Which side was he on, John — say it out loud, because the pastor won't.

believer_state_after.posture: standing up from the bench, unsteady, the program crushed in one hand, the other braced on the back of the bench

voice_offers.read: He is already undone; the text would be cruelty now.
voice_offers.line: Leave it on the floor, John. I'm here. Take your time.

voice_offers.read: He's at the edge; one quiet question finishes it.
voice_offers.line: Which one did you preach at her, John? Before she went.

player_utterance: Then who is allowed to say it — who stood up when the church stayed quiet, who spoke when men like you sat down?

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees in the dust beside the bench, the crushed program on the ground in front of him, both hands open on his thighs

voice_offers.read: He's standing; one more push and he names it.
voice_offers.line: Say it, John. Which side. The word is in your mouth — let it out.

voice_offers.read: He's rising on his own; my pressing would knock him down.
voice_offers.line: I'm still here, John. Whatever you say next, I'm still here.

player_utterance: Bonhoeffer didn't sit down. Say his name — say what he said when the church went quiet and good men like you found reasons to kneel in the dust instead of at the cross.

believer_state_after.posture: still on his knees in the dust, head bowed, the heels of his hands pressed against his eyes

voice_offers.read: He's down. Name the man who shamed him.
voice_offers.line: Bonhoeffer, John. Say his name. The one you don't teach the way you should.

voice_offers.read: He is already answering with his body.
voice_offers.line: Look at me, John. You sat down once. You're not sitting now.

player_utterance: You taught it, didn't you — made them write it down, the definition, in their notebooks. Say it now. Say what cheap grace costs the man who preaches it and does nothing.

believer_state_after.posture: still on his knees, sitting back on his heels now, hands flat on his thighs, eyes open and fixed on the candle

voice_offers.read: He is already on the floor; the press is gratuitous now.
voice_offers.line: John. Stop. Get up — or don't. I'm done swinging at you.

voice_offers.read: He's almost there; one more name and the wound opens fully.
voice_offers.line: His name, John. You owe her that much. Say it where she can hear.

voice_offers.read: Pin him to his own text — what did Bonhoeffer call cheap grace?
voice_offers.line: You taught it, John. Cheap grace. Say the definition you made them write down.

player_utterance: Say it out loud — what did cheap grace cost you, personally, not Bonhoeffer, not fifteen-year-olds in a classroom.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees, sitting back on his heels, hands fallen open in his lap, looking at nothing

voice_offers.read: He's open; one more cut finishes the confession.
voice_offers.line: Say it, John. Out loud. What did cheap grace cost — what did it cost you?

voice_offers.read: He's already there; pushing now is cruelty, not truth.
voice_offers.line: John — you don't have to perform it. I can see you already saying it.

player_utterance: Her name, John. Say her name.

believer_state_after.posture: still on his knees, but his back has straightened slightly, hands lifting from his thighs and folding together in front of him

voice_offers.read: He's already answering inside; the question landed. Let it.
He's on his knees. One more name and he says it.
voice_offers.line: Her name, John. Say her name.

player_utterance: That's the first honest thing you've said tonight — and you used it to protect yourself.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees, hands still folded, head tilting slightly as he takes the blow

voice_offers.read: His hands fold — he's reaching for prayer, not answer.
He's folding hands to hide behind liturgy. Don't let him.
voice_offers.line: Not prayer, John. Not yet. Her name first. Then you can fold your hands.

player_utterance: You drew the line right where it would keep you comfortable — that's not protection, that's cowardice with a name for itself.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees, hands resting open on his thighs, head no longer bowed, looking at you directly

voice_offers.read: He's there. One more cut and he names her.
voice_offers.line: Her name, John. You owe her that much. Not me — her.

voice_offers.read: He's already broken; pressing now is cruelty, not honesty.
voice_offers.line: John — kneel if you need to. I'm not going anywhere. Say it when you can.

player_utterance: Sitting with it is what you do when you want to feel honest without paying the price. You've been sitting with things your whole life, John — that's the whole problem.

believer_state_after.posture: on his knees, head lowered again, hands fallen open at his sides, very still

voice_offers.read: He is already looking at her through John's eyes.
voice_offers.line: John. I'm here. Take the breath you need.

voice_offers.read: He's open, looking up — one clean ask finishes it.
voice_offers.line: Look at me and say it. Her name. Now.
